body.body:
From Akien 2026-04-15 morning, after 36 PR_GOAL_STRATEGIC_20260414* test-pollution facia were found in the live DB contributing to slow queries and Igor retrieval confusion.

## Rule

All test data that should be thrown away gets four mechanisms:

1. **Tag** — metadata.test_data=True. The hook for cleanup queries.
2. **Tree path** — nodes appear under PR_IGORS_PROJECT.development.test_data subtree for human browsing; also reachable via a flat TEST_DATA_INDEX tree for single-query cleanup. (A node can be in multiple trees — D257.)
3. **TTL** — short half-life (default 1 hour, tunable). Orphans from crashed test runs self-degrade even if cleanup misses them. Biomimetic: biology gets self-degradation for free via protein half-life + autophagy; the test_data tag is the ubiquitin marker.
4. **pytest session fixture** — active enzyme. Autouse fixture in conftest.py sets IGOR_TEST_MODE=1, then deletes matching rows at session end.

Audit check = orphan detector for any test_data=True older than its TTL.

## Mechanism sketch

- Env var `IGOR_TEST_MODE=1` set by conftest autouse fixture before any test imports cortex
- cortex.store() / pr_create() / goal_adopt() / other creators check env var; when set, they stamp `test_data=True` + reparent to `TEST_DATA_ROOT` (or PR_IGORS_PROJECT.development.test_data subtree) automatically — so test authors don't need to remember
- Session-end fixture runs: `DELETE FROM memories WHERE metadata @> '{"test_data": true}'` (via jsonb_exists + boolean check per the db_proxy convention)
- TTL reaper: verify whether memories already have a TTL mechanism (twm_observations do via ttl_seconds; memories proper may not — check and decide: extend existing OR add minimal `expires_at` column + reaper pass that runs in sleep consolidation)
- Audit check: flag any test_data=True memory older than TTL as 'orphan test data — fixture or crash left this behind'
- Opt-out: tests that deliberately want persistent cross-session state set metadata.test_data=False explicitly

## Scope

1. conftest.py — add autouse session fixture that sets IGOR_TEST_MODE env var and runs cleanup at teardown
2. Seed TEST_DATA_ROOT facia node (or equivalent under PR_IGORS_PROJECT.development.test_data)
3. Wrap creator functions to auto-tag under IGOR_TEST_MODE (probably at cortex.store level — single choke point)
4. Add TTL support to memories if missing — or document that the session fixture is sufficient and TTL is a follow-up
5. Audit check for orphan test data
6. Migrate existing test suites that create facia (pr_consolidation_source, goal_formation, etc.) to use the new pattern — most should get it for free via the cortex.store wrapper

## Out of scope

- Retroactive conversion of existing permanent test fixtures that deliberately want persistence
- A full TTL/reaper infrastructure if the simplest version (session fixture) is sufficient

## Tests

- Session fixture sets env var and cleans up at end
- cortex.store stamps test_data=True when env var set
- cortex.store does NOT stamp when env var unset (no regression to prod path)
- DELETE query removes tagged rows, leaves untagged alone
- Audit check flags orphan test_data rows past TTL
- Opt-out path: explicit test_data=False survives cleanup

## Provenance

Akien proposed after 36 pr_consolidation_source test leftovers surfaced in pollution cleanup 2026-04-15. Biomimetic framing: tag = ubiquitin, tree = lysosome, TTL = protein half-life, fixture = active autophagy.
body.title: Test-data tag + TTL + auto-cleanup so throwaway fixtures self-degrade
body.result: test_data_lifecycle.py + conftest autouse session fixture + cortex.store auto-tag hook + goal_graph workaround. End-to-end verified: full suite 2371 pass, 0 tagged rows after run, 6 facia baseline preserved. FK-aware cleanup (memory_embeddings + trees) in dependent order. Follow-up T-goal-graph-use-cortex-store filed.
